steps.title: Expand both sides to standard form
Set condition for infinitely many solutions
steps.content:
For an equation in $x$ to have infinitely many solutions, it must be an identity — the same expression on both sides. The coefficients of $x$ already match ($7a = 7a$), so the constant terms must be equal:

$$-9a = -14a - 35$$

steps.title: Solve for the constant a
steps.content:
Add $14a$ to both sides:
$$-9a + 14a = -35$$
$$5a = -35$$
$$a = -7$$

Verification: when $a = -7$, both sides simplify to $-49x + 63$, confirming the identity. That matches choice B — B is correct.
